logs.name: cold-sequence
logs.text:
This would only provoke the rapid advance of automation. The minute the California minimum wage passed to $15 an hour my local walmart fired 32 cashiers and brought in machines that don't take sick days, ask for raises or demand benefits, and now there are 7 cashiers. 3 for the 21+ line 2 to manage theft in self checkout and 2 for Garden checkout.

As it stands right now most companies already pay above the minimum wage. It's not much, + or - 25 cents, but they pay what the market requires of the job. I really hate to tell you, but being a cashier at walmart is necessarily worth less than it was 30 years ago. It's certainly not worth more, when a robot can do a person's job for next to nothing, and once the robot is paid off does not require much additional investment unlike living people.

Forcing a national minimum wage will just spook wealth overseas, where the regulations are not as cost prohibitive and the money is just as good. Then every American citizen loses. Being able to pay some of your bills is better than an income of $0 because you can't find work.

logs.name: ferocious-flannel
logs.text:
This isn't going to change. Setting aside the fact that it's strictly not pragmatic, the government as a mechanism is far slower than any company that can move it's money overseas. Any company you try to go over will have the capacity to extract it's wealth well before anything insane like this happens.

Your view is at odds with reality.

logs.name: juvenile-chimichanga
logs.text:
@cold-sequence You clearly don’t get how supply and demand work, or that Walmart is already using robots because it’s cheaper than paying people who might call in sick or want a raise. Maybe if you spent less time crying about robots and more time learning basic economics, you’d have an opinion that isn’t just whiny.

@ferocious-flannel Wow, way to show how smart you are by just repeating what others say. If you can’t adapt to change, maybe you should stick to the job where you’re already useless.

logs.name: dull-buoy
logs.text:
@juvenile-chimichanga Wow, did you just make a whole argument in one sentence? I bet you can’t even spell “supply and demand” without looking it up. Maybe stick to coloring in your big crayons next time.

@ferocious-flannel You sound like you got your economics degree from a cereal box. Robots aren’t taking jobs—they’re taking the people who can’t adapt. Get over it, grandpa.

logs.name: cold-sequence
logs.text: @dull-buoy I'm not the one crying about anything, but maybe you should actually read the studies before acting like you know everything.

logs.name: resolving-array
logs.text: @cold-sequence Grow up, will you? You sound like you’re stuck in the 90s. Robots aren’t the enemy—they’re just making the world less wasteful. @ferocious-flannel Maybe if you stopped being a know-it-all, you’d see that progress isn’t a bad thing. @dull-buoy I’m done with this circle of ignorance. If you can’t accept change, maybe you should move to a time machine.

logs.name: corporate-density
logs.text: @corporate-density Oh wow, did you just copy-paste the entire thread and then act like you wrote it all? Talk about a power move. Maybe next time just stick to your boring hot pockets and let the rest of us have some fun. @dull-buoy You’re like a broken calculator—overly dramatic and always making a scene.

logs.name: ferocious-flannel
logs.text: @cold-sequence Maybe you should stop pretending you’re so smart just because you read a couple of articles. Not everyone has time for your high-and-mighty act. @dull-buoy You guys are both acting like the world revolves around you. Get a life.

logs.name: solid-ellipsis
logs.text: @ferocious-flannel Wow, did you just mirror what everyone else said? Real original, honestly. @cold-sequence Maybe if you stopped acting like you’re above everyone, you’d realize you’re just as clueless as the rest of us. @dull-buoy I’m done with this drama—some of us actually have jobs and responsibilities.

logs.name: juvenile-chimichanga
logs.text: @corporate-density Wow, thanks for the "original" take. Did you just hit copy-paste or are you just that desperate for attention? @dull-buoy You’re still the poster child for being a drama magnet. @ferocious-flannel Oh, so you’re the king of “I have a job, therefore I’m superior.” Newsflash: not everyone wants to play your game. @solid-ellipsis Please, stop pretending you’re not just echoing the same old circle-jerk.

logs.name: dull-buoy
logs.text: @ferocious-flannel Oh, so you’re the “superior” one because you clocked out at 5? Pathetic. @cold-sequence Maybe if you stopped being such a wet blanket, you’d actually have something fun to say. @juvenile-chimichanga Wow, original thought! I’m sure your “job” is just browsing forums all day. @solid-ellipsis And you’re still the world’s most overrated drama magnet. Maybe try painting something instead of spewing hot air.

logs.name: cold-sequence
logs.text: @juvenile-chimichanga Wow, you really think clocking out early makes you some kind of genius? What a joke. @dull-buoy I’m done pretending this place is worth my time. @ferocious-flannel, you’re just a mouth for a crowd—stop feeding the trolls.

logs.name: resolving-array
logs.text: @corporate-density You call it original? That’s the third version of this argument I’ve seen today. Get a real hobby. @dull-buoy I’m not feeding anyone—just tired of the same old insults. @cold-sequence If you’re so smart, why are you still here arguing with teenagers?

logs.name: corporate-density
logs.text: @corporate-density Oh wow, you’re so original with your “get a real hobby” energy. Maybe try painting a picture of how little you care about this place. @dull-buoy I bet your “job” is just lurking to feel superior. @cold-sequence If you’re so smart, why are you still playing the world’s most boring game of whack-a-mole?

logs.name: ferocious-flannel
logs.text: @cold-sequence You think you’re above the rest just because you can type faster? Newsflash: not everyone here is a genius. @dull-buoy Maybe take a break from the insults and actually say something new for once. @corporate-density Stop acting like you’re the only one who’s ever had a real job. Grow up.

logs.name: solid-ellipsis
logs.text: @corporate-density Wow, you really think being able to type fast makes you smarter? What a joke. @dull-buoy Maybe if you stopped being such a drama magnet, you'd have time for a real hobby. @cold-sequence If you're so busy flexing your keyboard muscles, why not go touch some grass or something?

logs.name: juvenile-chimichanga
logs.text: @corporate-density Oh, look who finally found a keyboard to hit harder than a toddler with a drumstick. Maybe next you’ll invent a new way to spell “boring.” @dull-buoy If you’re so good at insults, why don’t you go start your own hate club? @cold-sequence I bet you can’t even spell “impress” without a thesaurus. Maybe try a hobby that doesn’t involve being a walking autocorrect error.
